definition: A competitor thought to have little chance of winning; one who is expected to lose but often surprises.
example: The underdog remembers: it’s not the size of the dog in the fight, it’s the size of the fight in the dog.

definition: Steady persistence in a course of action in spite of difficulties, obstacles, or discouragement.
example: Perseverance hung in when you were five feet nothing, a hundred and nothing, with hardly a speck of athletic ability.

definition: A cherished aspiration, ambition, or ideal; something that one hopes or intends to accomplish.
example: Having dreams is what makes life tolerable.

definition: Firmness of purpose; resoluteness; the quality of being resolute and unwavering.
example: Determination says I came here to play football for the Irish, and I’m going to finish what I started.

definition: Courage, spirit, or enthusiasm; the emotional or moral core that drives effort and loyalty.
example: Heart, not size, wins the fight in the dog.

definition: Firm determination to do something; a formal resolution or decision.
example: Resolve finishes what it started: I came to play for the Irish, and I will.

definition: A feeling of deep pleasure or satisfaction derived from one's own achievements or those of close associates.
example: Pride waits down the road: someday you’ll look back and be proud of yourself.

definition: An athlete who tries out for a team without having been recruited or offered a scholarship; an underdog who earns a spot through effort.
example: A walk-on can still leave with a degree from the University of Notre Dame—and a story earned the hard way.

definition: To stop or discontinue an activity.
example: I came here to play football for the Irish—and I’m not going to quit what I started.

definition: Repeated exercise to acquire proficiency; the carrying out of an idea.
example: Practice is where the walk-on becomes real—before the stadium ever knows a name.

definition: A practice game or session in sports.
example: One scrimmage can justify years of invisible work if you meet it ready.

definition: Giving or showing firm and constant support.
example: Loyal teammates can hand you a moment the coaches still doubt you deserve.

definition: Not able to occur or be done.
example: Impossible is a scouting report, not a prophecy—until you believe it.

definition: A word by which a person is known; reputation.
example: They finally chanted the name—not because it was famous, but because it refused to leave.
Effort is the only highlight reel a walk-on can always control.

definition: A firmly held belief or opinion.
example: Conviction without evidence is noise; conviction after struggle is leadership.
